info.title: TensorZero Gateway API
info.description: TensorZero Gateway — inference, feedback, and observability API

post.summary:
This handler starts a batch inference request for a particular function.
The entire batch must use the same function and variant.
It will fail if we fail to kick off the batch request for any reason.
However, the batch request might still fail for other reasons after it has been started.

schema.description:
The expected payload to the `/start_batch_inference` endpoint.
It will be a JSON object with the following fields:

get.summary:
Polls a batch inference request that was made using the `/start_batch_inference` endpoint
Semantics: if the batch is pending, it will actually poll the model provider
If the batch is failed, it will return a failed response immediately
If the batch is completed, it will return the appropriate response immediately from the database

Polls a single inference within a batch inference request.
This is a thin wrapper around `poll_batch_inference_handler` that converts the path params.

parameters.name: inference_id
parameters.description: A specific inference ID within the batch

post.summary: A handler for the feedback endpoint

get.summary: A handler for a health check that includes availability of related services

post.summary: A handler for the inference endpoint

include_original_response.description:
DEPRECATED (#5697 / 2026.4+): Use `include_raw_response` instead.
If `true`, add an `original_response` field to the response, containing the raw string response from the model.
Note that for complex variants (e.g. `experimental_best_of_n_sampling`), the response may not contain `original_response`
if the fuser/judge model failed.

include_raw_response.description:
If `true`, add a `raw_response` field to the response, containing the raw string response from the model.
Note that for complex variants (e.g. `experimental_best_of_n_sampling`), the response may not contain `raw_response`
if the fuser/judge model failed.

include_raw_usage.description: If `true`, include `raw_usage` in the response's `usage` field, containing the raw usage data from each model inference.

include_aggregated_response.description: If `true`, include an `aggregated_response` field in the final chunk in the stream

schema.description: The expected payload is a JSON object with the following fields:

post.summary: Handler for `POST /internal/action`
post.description: Executes an inference, feedback, or evaluation action using a historical config snapshot.

get.description:
Returns whether autopilot is configured (i.e., whether `TENSORZERO_AUTOPILOT_API_KEY` is set).
This endpoint does not require authentication and does not make any external calls.
Lists sessions from the Autopilot API.

schema.description: Response from listing sessions.

post.summary: Handler for `POST /internal/autopilot/v1/sessions/{session_id}/actions/approve_all`
post.description:
Approves all pending tool calls for a session via the Autopilot API.
The deployment_id and tensorzero_version are injected from the gateway's app state.

schema.description:
HTTP request body for approving all pending tool calls.

This is the request type used by the HTTP handler. The `deployment_id` and
`tensorzero_version` are injected from the gateway's app state.

last_tool_call_event_id.description:
Only approve tool calls with event IDs <= this value.
Prevents race condition where new tool calls arrive after client fetched the list.

schema.description: Response from approving all pending tool calls.

approved_count.description: Number of tool calls that were approved.

event_ids.description: Event IDs of the newly created ToolCallAuthorization events.

tool_call_event_ids.description: Event IDs of the tool calls that were approved.

post.summary: Handler for `POST /internal/autopilot/v1/sessions/{session_id}/actions/interrupt`
post.description: Interrupts an autopilot session via the Autopilot API.

post.summary: Handler for `POST /internal/autopilot/v1/sessions/{session_id}/aws/s3_initiate_upload`
post.description: Initiates an S3 upload via the Autopilot API.

schema.description:
HTTP request body for initiating an S3 upload.

This is the request type used by the HTTP handler.
Response from initiating an S3 upload, containing temporary credentials.

get.description: Lists config writes (write_config tool calls) for a session from the Autopilot API.

schema.description:
Response from listing config writes as seen by gateway consumers.

Uses `GatewayEvent` which excludes `NotAvailable` authorization status.

get.description: Lists events for a session from the Autopilot API.

schema.description:
Response from listing events as seen by gateway consumers.

Uses `GatewayEvent` which excludes `NotAvailable` authorization status.

previous_user_message_event_id.description: The most recent `message` event with role `user` in this session.

status.description:
The current status of the Autopilot in this session.
Ignores pagination parameters.

pending_tool_calls.description:
All tool calls in Event history that do not have responses.
These may be duplicates of some of the values in events.
All EventPayloads in these Events should be of type ToolCall.

pending_user_questions.description: All user_questions events that do not have a matching user_questions_answers event.

post.summary: Handler for `POST /internal/autopilot/v1/sessions/{session_id}/events`
post.description:
Creates an event in a session via the Autopilot API.
The deployment_id is injected from the gateway's app state.
The `tensorzero-beta-tools` header, if present, is forwarded to the remote server.

schema.description:
HTTP request body for creating an event.

This is the request type used by the HTTP handler. The `deployment_id` is
injected from the gateway's app state, so it's not included in this request.

previous_user_message_event_id.description: Used for idempotency when adding events to an existing session.

schema.description: Response from creating an event.

get.description:
Streams events for a session via SSE from the Autopilot API.
Note: The #[instrument] macro is not used here due to lifetime issues with the SSE stream.
Returns the live config snapshot.

post.summary: Handler for `POST /internal/config`
post.description:
Writes a config snapshot to the database and returns its hash.
If a config with the same hash already exists, tags are merged
(new tags override existing keys) and created_at is preserved.

The config is validated by running the full config loading pipeline
(with credential validation disabled) before writing. This catches
issues like invalid model references, missing templates, and
cross-reference errors that serde deserialization alone would miss.

schema.description: Request body for writing a config snapshot.

extra_templates.description: Templates that should be stored with the config.

tags.description:
User-defined tags for categorizing this config snapshot.
Tags are merged with any existing tags for the same config hash.

get.description: Returns a config snapshot by hash.

get.summary:
Handler for the GET `/internal/datasets` endpoint.
Returns metadata for all datasets with optional filtering and pagination.

post.summary:
The handler for the POST `/internal/datasets/{dataset_name}/datapoints/clone` endpoint.
This endpoint clones datapoints to a target dataset, preserving all fields except id and dataset_name.

post.summary: HTTP handler for querying episodes (POST with filters)

schema.description: Request body for the POST episode table endpoint

limit.description: Maximum number of episodes to return (max 100)

before.description: Return episodes before this episode_id (for pagination)

after.description: Return episodes after this episode_id (for pagination)

function_name.description: Optional function name to filter episodes by

oneOf.description:
Optional filter to apply when querying episodes.
Episodes are returned if they have at least one inference matching the filter.

HTTP handler for getting inference counts for an episode

get.description: Returns the count of unique datapoints across the specified evaluation runs.

Checks if human feedback exists for a given combination of metric name,
datapoint ID, and output.

schema.description: Request body for checking human feedback.

metric_name.description: The name of the metric being evaluated.

output.description: The serialized inference output to match against.

get.description: Returns information about evaluation runs associated with a specific datapoint.
Returns paginated evaluation results across one or more evaluation runs.

post.description: Runs an evaluation and streams results via SSE.

get.description: Returns information about specific evaluation runs.
Returns a paginated list of evaluation runs across all functions.
Returns the total count of unique evaluation runs across all functions.
Searches evaluation runs by ID or variant name.

Returns aggregated statistics (mean, confidence intervals) for the specified
evaluation runs and metrics.

post.summary: HTTP handler for the count inferences endpoint.

schema.description: Request to count inferences matching the given parameters.

function_name.description: Optional function name to filter inferences by.

variant_name.description: Optional variant name to filter inferences by.

episode_id.description: Optional episode ID to filter inferences by.

output_source.description:
Source of the inference output. When set to "demonstration", only inferences
with demonstration feedback will be counted.

oneOf.description: Optional filter to apply when counting inferences.

search_query_experimental.description: Experimental: search query to filter inferences by.

get.summary: HTTP handler for getting model inferences by inference ID

get.description: Returns the count of distinct models that have been used.
Returns model latency quantile distributions.
Returns model usage timeseries data (tokens, counts over time).

get.summary:
Fetches an object using the object store and path specified by the encoded `StoragePath`.
This does not need to match the gateway's current object store (e.g. a `StorageKind::Filesystem`)
could be provided even if the gateway is configured with `StorageKind::S3Compatible`.
However, if the provider requires authentication, the gateway must have the correct credentials
set as environment variables.
get.description:
This is invoked as `GET /internal/object_storage?storage_path=<urlencoded_storagepath>`.
The `<urlencoded_storagepath>` value constructed by serializing `StoragePath` to JSON,
and the urlencoding the resulting string.
For example, `GET /internal/object_storage?storage_path={%22kind%22:{%22type%22:%22filesystem%22,%22path%22:%22/tmp%22},%22path%22:%22fake-tensorzero-file%22}`

schema.description: Response type for the resolve_uuid endpoint.

get.description: Returns a UI-safe subset of the Config.
Returns a UI-safe subset of the Config for a historical config snapshot.

Returns a list of workflow evaluation run episodes grouped by task_name.
Episodes with NULL task_name are grouped individually.
Returns the count of distinct episode groups (by task_name) for the given run IDs.
Gets workflow evaluation runs by their IDs.
Returns a paginated list of workflow evaluation runs with episode counts.
Returns a paginated list of workflow evaluation projects.
Gets workflow evaluation run episodes with their feedback for a specific run.
Returns the total count of episodes for a given workflow evaluation run.

Gets aggregated statistics (count, mean, stdev, confidence intervals) for a workflow
evaluation run, grouped by metric name.
Returns the total count of workflow evaluation runs.
Searches workflow evaluation runs by project_name and/or search_query.

get.summary: A handler for a simple liveness check

post.summary:
Handler for the POST `/v1/datasets/{dataset_id}/datapoints` endpoint.
Creates manual datapoints in a dataset.

schema.description:
Request to create datapoints manually.
Used by the `POST /v1/datasets/{dataset_id}/datapoints` endpoint.
Request to delete datapoints from a dataset.

ids.description: The IDs of the datapoints to delete.

schema.description: Request to update one or more datapoints in a dataset.

Request to update metadata for one or more datapoints in a dataset.
Used by the `PATCH /v1/datasets/{dataset_id}/datapoints/metadata` endpoint.

datapoints.description: The datapoints to update metadata for.

post.summary:
Handler for the POST `/v1/datasets/{dataset_id}/from_inferences` endpoint.
Creates datapoints from inferences based on either specific inference IDs or an inference query.

schema.description: Request to create datapoints from inferences.

post.summary:
Handler for the POST `/v1/datasets/{dataset_name}/get_datapoints` endpoint.
Retrieves datapoints scoped to a dataset by their IDs.

schema.description:
Request to get specific datapoints by their IDs.
Used by the `POST /v1/datasets/{dataset_name}/get_datapoints` endpoint.

ids.description: The IDs of the datapoints to retrieve. Required.

post.summary:
Handler for the POST `/v1/datasets/{dataset_id}/list_datapoints` endpoint.
Lists datapoints from a dataset with optional filtering and pagination.

schema.description:
Request to list datapoints from a dataset with pagination and filters.
Used by the `POST /v1/datasets/{dataset_id}/list_datapoints` endpoint.

function_name.description:
Optional function name to filter datapoints by.
If provided, only datapoints from this function will be returned.

limit.description:
The maximum number of datapoints to return.
Defaults to 20.

page_size.description:
The maximum number of datapoints to return. Defaults to 20.
Deprecated: please use `limit`. If `limit` is provided, `page_size` is ignored.

offset.description:
The number of datapoints to skip before starting to return results.
Defaults to 0.

oneOf.description:
Optional filter to apply when querying datapoints.
Supports filtering by tags, time, and logical combinations (AND/OR/NOT).

order_by.description:
Optional ordering criteria for the results.
Supports multiple sort criteria (e.g., sort by timestamp then by search relevance).

search_query_experimental.description:
Text query to filter. Case-insensitive substring search over the datapoints' input and output.

THIS FEATURE IS EXPERIMENTAL, and we may change or remove it at any time.
We recommend against depending on this feature for critical use cases.

Important limitations:
- This requires an exact substring match; we do not tokenize this query string.
- This doesn't search for any content in the template itself.
- Quality is based on term frequency > 0, without any relevance scoring.
- There are no performance guarantees (it's best effort only). Today, with no other
  filters, it will perform a full table scan, which may be extremely slow depending
  on the data volume.

post.summary:
Handler for the POST `/v1/inferences/get_inferences` endpoint.
Retrieves specific inferences by their IDs.

schema.description:
Request to get specific inferences by their IDs.
Used by the `POST /v1/inferences/get_inferences` endpoint.

ids.description: The IDs of the inferences to retrieve. Required.

function_name.description:
Optional function name to filter by.
Including this improves query performance since `function_name` is the first column
in the ClickHouse primary key.

output_source.description:
Source of the inference output.
Determines whether to return the original inference output or demonstration feedback
(manually-curated output) if available.
Defaults to `Inference` if not specified.

post.summary:
Handler for the POST `/v1/inferences/list_inferences` endpoint.
Lists inferences with optional filtering, pagination, and sorting.

schema.description:
Request to list inferences with pagination and filters.
Used by the `POST /v1/inferences/list_inferences` endpoint.

function_name.description:
Optional function name to filter inferences by.
If provided, only inferences from this function will be returned.

variant_name.description:
Optional variant name to filter inferences by.
If provided, only inferences from this variant will be returned.

episode_id.description:
Optional episode ID to filter inferences by.
If provided, only inferences from this episode will be returned.

output_source.description:
Source of the inference output. Determines whether to return the original
inference output or demonstration feedback (manually-curated output) if available.
Defaults to `Inference` if not specified.

limit.description:
The maximum number of inferences to return.
Defaults to 20.

offset.description:
The number of inferences to skip before starting to return results.
Defaults to 0.

before.description:
Optional inference ID to paginate before (exclusive).
Returns inferences with IDs before this one (earlier in time).
Cannot be used together with `after` or `offset`.

after.description:
Optional inference ID to paginate after (exclusive).
Returns inferences with IDs after this one (later in time).
Cannot be used together with `before` or `offset`.

oneOf.description:
Optional filter to apply when querying inferences.
Supports filtering by metrics, tags, time, and logical combinations (AND/OR/NOT).

order_by.description:
Optional ordering criteria for the results.
Supports multiple sort criteria (e.g., sort by timestamp then by metric).

search_query_experimental.description:
Text query to filter. Case-insensitive substring search over the inferences' input and output.

THIS FEATURE IS EXPERIMENTAL, and we may change or remove it at any time.
We recommend against depending on this feature for critical use cases.

Important limitations:
- This requires an exact substring match; we do not tokenize this query string.
- This doesn't search for any content in the template itself.
- Quality is based on term frequency > 0, without any relevance scoring.
- There are no performance guarantees (it's best effort only). Today, with no other
  filters, it will perform a full table scan, which may be extremely slow depending
  on the data volume.

get.summary: HTTP handler for the variant sampling probabilities endpoint (query-based)

ApiType.description:
The type of API used for a model inference.
Used in raw usage reporting to help consumers interpret provider-specific usage data.

Arguments.description: A newtype wrapper around Map<String, Value> for template and system arguments

AutopilotSideInfo.description:
Side information required for autopilot client tools.

This should contain all IDs that might be needed as input to a tool
that do not need to be generated by LLMs (like the session id or a config hash).
We should implement this as a type that has optional or mandatory fields as needed
for each kind of tool

tool_call_event_id.description: The event ID of the ToolCall event (for correlating ToolResult).

session_id.description: The session ID for this autopilot session.

config_snapshot_hash.description: A hash of the current configuration.

optimization.description: Settings for optimization workflows run on the gateway by autopilot.

AutopilotStatus.description: The UX-relevant status of the Autopilot.

AutopilotStatusResponse.description:
Response for the autopilot status endpoint.

Indicates whether the autopilot client is configured (i.e., whether
`TENSORZERO_AUTOPILOT_API_KEY` is set).

Base64File.description: A file already encoded as base64

BatchInferenceParams.description:
InferenceParams is the top-level struct for inference parameters.
We backfill these from the configs given in the variants used and ultimately write them to the database.

ChatEvaluationResultRow.description: Evaluation result for a chat function.

evaluator_inference_id.description: The evaluator inference ID, if the feedback was generated by an LLM judge

oneOf.description: The input to the function

generated_output.description: The generated output from the model

reference_output.description: The reference output from the datapoint

metric_value.description: The metric value as a string (could be boolean or float)

feedback_id.description: The feedback ID for this metric

variant_name.description: The variant name used for this inference

staled_at.description: When the datapoint was marked as stale (if ever)

ChatInferenceDatapoint.description:
Wire variant of ChatInferenceDatapoint for API responses with Python/TypeScript bindings
This one should be used in all public interfaces.

ContentBlockChatOutput.description: Defines the types of content block that can come from a `chat` function

ContentBlockOutput.description: Types of content blocks that can be returned by a model provider

CountInferencesResponse.description: Response containing the count of matching inferences.

count.description: The count of inferences matching the query parameters.

CountModelsResponse.description: Response containing the count of distinct models used.

model_count.description: The count of distinct models used.

CountWorkflowEvaluationRunEpisodesByTask.description: Response containing the count of distinct episodes by task_name.

CountWorkflowEvaluationRunEpisodesRespon.description: Response containing the count of episodes for a workflow evaluation run.

CountWorkflowEvaluationRunsResponse.description: Response containing the count of workflow evaluation runs.

allOf.description:
Dynamic tool parameters for the datapoint. Optional.
This is flattened to mirror inference requests.

function_name.description: The function name for this datapoint. Required.

episode_id.description: Episode ID that the datapoint belongs to. Optional.

tags.description: Tags associated with this datapoint. Optional.

name.description: Human-readable name for the datapoint. Optional.

inference_ids.description: The inference IDs to create datapoints from.

oneOf.description: JSON datapoint output. Optional.

The output schema of the JSON datapoint. Optional.

name.description: Human-readable name for the datapoint. Optional.

Wire variant of Datapoint enum for API responses with Python/TypeScript bindings
This one should be used in all public interfaces.

DatapointFilter.description:
Filter tree for querying datapoints.
This is similar to `InferenceFilter` but without metric filters, as datapoints don't have associated metrics.

DatapointMetadataUpdate.description: A request to update the metadata of a datapoint.

name.description: Datapoint name. If omitted, it will be left unchanged. If specified as `null`, it will be set to `null`. If specified as a value, it will be set to the provided value.

allOf.description: The property to order by.

DatapointOrderBy.description: Order by clauses for querying datapoints.

DatapointOrderByTerm.description:
The property to order datapoints by.
This is flattened in the public API inside the `DatapointOrderBy` struct.

DatapointStatsResponse.description: Response containing the count of unique datapoints.

datapoint_count.description: The total number of datapoints in the dataset.

last_updated.description: The timestamp of the last update (ISO 8601 format).

DeleteDatapointsResponse.description: Response containing the number of deleted datapoints.

Detail.description: Detail level for input images (affects fidelity and token cost)

DynamicToolParams.description:
Wire/API representation of dynamic tool parameters for inference requests.

This type is the **wire format** for tool configurations used in API requests and responses.
It distinguishes between static tools (configured in the function) and dynamic tools
(provided at runtime), allowing clients to reference pre-configured tools by name or
provide new tools on-the-fly.

# Purpose
- Accept tool parameters in inference API requests (e.g., `/inference/{function_name}`)
- Expose tool configurations in API responses for stored inferences
- Support Python and TypeScript client bindings
- Allow runtime customization of tool behavior

# Fields
- `allowed_tools`: Names of static tools from function config to use (subset selection)
- `additional_tools`: New tools defined at runtime (not in static config)
- `tool_choice`: Override the function's default tool choice strategy
- `parallel_tool_calls`: Override whether parallel tool calls are enabled
- `provider_tools`: Provider-specific tool configurations (not persisted to database)

# Key Differences from ToolCallConfigDatabaseInsert
- **Separate lists**: Maintains distinction between static (`allowed_tools`) and dynamic (`additional_tools`) tools
- **By reference**: Static tools referenced by name, not duplicated
- **Has provider_tools**: Can specify provider-specific tool configurations
- **Has bindings**: Exposed to Python/TypeScript via `pyo3` and `ts_rs`

# Conversion to Storage Format
Converting from `DynamicToolParams` to `ToolCallConfigDatabaseInsert` is a **lossy** operation:
1. Static tools (from `allowed_tools` names) are resolved from function config
2. Dynamic tools (from `additional_tools`) are included as-is
3. Both lists are merged into a single `tools_available` list
4. The distinction between static and dynamic tools is lost
5. `provider_tools` are dropped (not stored)

Use `FunctionConfig::dynamic_tool_params_to_database_insert()` for this conversion.

# Conversion from Storage Format
Converting from `ToolCallConfigDatabaseInsert` back to `DynamicToolParams` reconstructs the original:
1. `dynamic_tools` -> `additional_tools`
2. `allowed_tools` -> `allowed_tools` (based on choice enum)
3. Other fields copied directly

Use `From<ToolCallConfigDatabaseInsert> for DynamicToolParams` for this conversion.

# Example
```rust,ignore
// API request with dynamic tool params
let params = DynamicToolParams {
    allowed_tools: Some(vec!["calculator".to_string()]),  // Use only the calculator tool from config
    additional_tools: Some(vec![Tool {  runtime tool  }]),  // Add a new tool
    tool_choice: Some(ToolChoice::Required),
    parallel_tool_calls: Some(true),
    provider_tools: vec![],
};

// Convert to storage format
let db_insert = function_config
    .dynamic_tool_params_to_database_insert(params, &static_tools)?
    .unwrap_or_default();

// db_insert.tools_available now contains both the calculator tool (from config)
// and the runtime tool (from additional_tools), merged together
```

See also: [`ToolCallConfigDatabaseInsert`] for the storage/database format

allowed_tools.description:
A subset of static tools configured for the function that the inference is allowed to use. Optional.
If not provided, all static tools are allowed.

additional_tools.description:
Tools that the user provided at inference time (not in function config), in addition to the function-configured
tools, that are also allowed.

oneOf.description:
User-specified tool choice strategy. If provided during inference, it will override the function-configured tool choice.
Optional.

parallel_tool_calls.description:
Whether to use parallel tool calls in the inference. Optional.
If provided during inference, it will override the function-configured parallel tool calls.

EvaluationResultRow.description: Evaluation result row that can represent either chat or JSON function output.

EvaluationRunInfo.description: Information about a single evaluation run.

EvaluationRunInfoById.description: Information about a single evaluation run (returned by get_evaluation_run_infos).

EvaluationRunStatsResponse.description: Response containing the count of evaluation runs.

count.description: The total count of evaluation runs.

EvaluationStatistics.description: Statistics for a single evaluation run and metric.

EventPayload.description:
Internal event payload type - consumers should use `GatewayEventPayload` instead.

Note: TS derive is needed for types that reference this, but we don't export it.

EventPayloadMessage.description:
A message payload specific to autopilot events.
Content is restricted to Text blocks only.

EventPayloadMessageContent.description:
Content block types allowed in autopilot event messages.
Restricted to only Text blocks (no ToolCall, File, Template, etc.).

resolved_uuids.description:
Attempted lookups for anything that matched a UUID regex
in the parent `EventPayloadMessage`

EventPayloadStatusUpdate.description: Status update payload for an event.

EventPayloadToolCall.description:
Autopilot tool call with side info for tool execution.

This extends the interface of a standard tool call with bookkeeping information that
allows the caller to send over non-llm generated parameters.

side_info.description: Side info to pass to the tool (hidden from LLM, used for execution context).

EventPayloadToolResult.description: Tool result payload for an event.

header.description: Very short label displayed as a chip/tag (max 12 chars). Examples: "Auth method", "Library", "Approach".

question.description: The complete question to ask the user. Should be clear, specific, and end with a question mark.

EventPayloadUserQuestion.description: A single question to display to the user.

EventPayloadUserQuestionInner.description: The format of a user question.

EventPayloadUserQuestionsAnswers.description: User responses payload for an event.

responses.description: Map from question UUID to response.

tool_execution_id.description:
The ID of the tool execution that generated this visualization.
For client-side tools, this is the ToolCall event ID.
For server-side tools, this is the task ID.

pointer.description: A JSON Pointer to the field to update (e.g. `/enable_agi`)

value.description: The value to set the field to

pointer.description: A JSON Pointer to the field to update (e.g. `/enable_agi`)

name.description: The name of the HTTP header (e.g. `anthropic-beta`)

variant_name.description: A variant name in your configuration (e.g. `my_variant`)

The name of the HTTP header (e.g. `anthropic-beta`)

File.description: A file for an inference or a datapoint.

FunctionInferenceCount.description: Row returned from the list_functions_with_inference_count query.

last_inference_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent inference for this function

inference_count.description: Total number of inferences for this function

FunctionTool.description:
`FunctionTool` is a particular kind of tool that relies
on the client to execute a function on their side (a ToolCall content block)
and return the result on the next turn (a ToolCallResult).
Notably, we assume there is a JSON schema `parameters` that specifies the
set of arguments that the tool will accept.

strict.description:
`strict` here specifies that TensorZero should attempt to use any facilities
available from the model provider to force the model to generate an accurate tool call,
notably OpenAI's strict tool call mode (https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling#strict-mode).
This imposes additional restrictions on the JSON schema that may vary across providers
so we allow it to be configurable.

GatewayEvent.description:
An event as seen by gateway consumers.

Uses `GatewayEventPayload` which excludes `NotAvailable` authorization status.

GatewayEventPayload.description:
Event payload as seen by gateway consumers.

Uses `GatewayEventPayloadToolCallAuthorization` which excludes `NotAvailable` status.

GatewayEventPayloadToolCallAuthorization.description:
Tool call authorization payload as seen by gateway consumers.

Uses `GatewayToolCallAuthorizationStatus` which excludes `NotAvailable`.

GatewayToolCallAuthorizationStatus.description:
Authorization status for tool calls as seen by gateway consumers.

This is a narrower type than `ToolCallAuthorizationStatus` that excludes
`NotAvailable` since that status is filtered out before reaching consumers.

config.description: The config in a form suitable for serialization.

hash.description: The hash identifying this config version.

extra_templates.description: Templates that were loaded from the filesystem.

tags.description: User-defined tags for categorizing or labeling this config snapshot.

datapoint_count.description: The count of datapoints for the dataset

GetFunctionThroughputByVariantResponse.description: Response containing function throughput data grouped by variant and time period

throughput.description: Throughput data for each (period, variant) combination

GetHumanFeedbackResponse.description: Response for the check human feedback endpoint.

oneOf.description: The human feedback result, if it exists.

GetModelLatencyResponse.description: Response containing model latency quantile data.

quantiles.description: The quantile inputs (e.g. [0.001, 0.005, ..., 0.999]) used to compute the distributions.

data.description: The model latency data points with quantile distributions.

GetModelUsageResponse.description: Response containing model usage timeseries data.

probabilities.description:
Map of variant names to their sampling probabilities (0.0 to 1.0)
Probabilities sum to 1.0

GetWorkflowEvaluationProjectCountRespons.description: Response containing the count of workflow evaluation projects.

GetWorkflowEvaluationProjectsResponse.description: Response containing a list of workflow evaluation projects.

GetWorkflowEvaluationRunEpisodesWithFeed.description: Response containing a list of workflow evaluation run episodes with feedback.

GetWorkflowEvaluationRunStatisticsRespon.description: Response containing statistics for a workflow evaluation run grouped by metric.

GetWorkflowEvaluationRunsResponse.description: Response containing a list of workflow evaluation runs by IDs.

GroupedWorkflowEvaluationRunEpisodeWithF.description:
A workflow evaluation run episode with feedback, including its group key.

The group_key is either the task_name or a generated key for episodes with NULL task_name.

group_key.description: The grouping key - either task_name or 'NULL_EPISODE_{episode_id_uint}'

run_id.description: The run ID this episode belongs to

task_name.description: The task name (datapoint_name). NULL for episodes without a task name.

feedback_metric_names.description: Metric names for feedback, sorted alphabetically

inference_count.description: Number of inferences for this variant

last_used_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last inference

inference_count.description: The count of inferences for the function (and optionally variant)

count_by_variant.description: Counts grouped by variant (only present when group_by=variant)

InferenceEvaluationHumanFeedbackRow.description:
Result of checking for existing human feedback for an inference evaluation.
This is used to determine if a human has already provided feedback for a
(metric_name, datapoint_id, output) combination, allowing the evaluation
system to use human feedback instead of running automated evaluators.

evaluator_inference_id.description: The inference ID associated with this feedback.

allOf.description: Filter by the value of a float metric

oneOf.title: InferenceFilterFloatMetric
oneOf.description: Filter by the value of a float metric

allOf.description: Filter by the value of a boolean metric

oneOf.title: InferenceFilterBooleanMetric
oneOf.description: Filter by the value of a boolean metric

allOf.description: Filter by whether an inference has a demonstration.

oneOf.title: InferenceFilterDemonstrationFeedback
oneOf.description: Filter by whether an inference has a demonstration.

allOf.description: Filter by tag key-value pair

oneOf.title: InferenceFilterTag
oneOf.description: Filter by tag key-value pair

allOf.description: Filter by the timestamp of an inference.

oneOf.title: InferenceFilterTime
oneOf.description: Filter by the timestamp of an inference.

oneOf.title: InferenceFilterAnd
oneOf.description: Logical AND of multiple filters

oneOf.title: InferenceFilterOr
oneOf.description: Logical OR of multiple filters

oneOf.title: InferenceFilterNot
oneOf.description: Logical NOT of a filter

InferenceMetadata.description: Inference metadata from the InferenceById table.

InferenceOutputSource.description:
Source of an inference output when querying inferences. Users can choose this because there may be
demonstration feedback (manually-curated output) for the inference that should be preferred.

InferenceParams.description:
InferenceParams is the top-level struct for inference parameters.
We backfill these from the configs given in the variants used and ultimately write them to the database.

InferenceResponse.description: InferenceResponse and InferenceResultChunk determine what gets serialized and sent to the client

InferenceResponseToolCall.description:
An InferenceResponseToolCall is a request by a model to call a Tool
in the form that we return to the client / ClickHouse
This includes some synactic sugar (parsing / validation of the tool arguments)
in the `arguments` field and the name in the `name` field.
We support looping this back through the TensorZero inference API via the ToolCallWrapper

id.description: A Tool Call ID to match up with tool call responses. See #4058.

raw_name.description: The name of the tool to call, as generated by the model.

raw_arguments.description: The raw arguments JSON string of the tool to call, as generated by the model.

name.description: The name of the tool to call, validated against tool configs. If not present, it means the tool call was invalid.

arguments.description: The arguments of the tool to call, validated against tool configs. If not present, it means the tool call arguments were invalid.

InferenceWithFeedbackCountResponse.description: Response containing inference count with feedback count

feedback_count.description: Number of feedbacks for the metric

inference_count.description: Number of inferences matching the metric threshold criteria

Input.description: API representation of an input to a model.

oneOf.description: System prompt of the input.

InputMessage.description:
InputMessage and Role are our representation of the input sent by the client
prior to any processing into LLM representations below.
`InputMessage` has a custom deserializer that addresses legacy data formats that we used to support (see input_message.rs).

allOf.description:
An unknown content block type, used to allow passing provider-specific
content blocks (e.g. Anthropic's `redacted_thinking`) in and out
of TensorZero.
The `data` field holds the original content block from the provider,
without any validation or transformation by TensorZero.

oneOf.title: InputMessageContentUnknown
oneOf.description:
An unknown content block type, used to allow passing provider-specific
content blocks (e.g. Anthropic's `redacted_thinking`) in and out
of TensorZero.
The `data` field holds the original content block from the provider,
without any validation or transformation by TensorZero.

JsonDatapointOutputUpdate.description:
A request to update the output of a JSON datapoint.

We intentionally only accept the `raw` field, because JSON datapoints can contain invalid or malformed JSON for eval purposes.

raw.description:
The raw output of the datapoint. For valid JSON outputs, this should be a JSON-serialized string.

This will be parsed and validated against the datapoint's `output_schema`. Valid `raw` values will be parsed and stored as `parsed`, and
invalid `raw` values will be stored as-is, because we allow invalid outputs in datapoints by design.

JsonEvaluationResultRow.description: Evaluation result for a JSON function.

evaluator_inference_id.description: The evaluator inference ID, if the feedback was generated by an LLM judge

oneOf.description: The input to the function
The generated output from the model
The reference output from the datapoint

metric_value.description: The metric value as a string (could be boolean or float)

feedback_id.description: The feedback ID for this metric

variant_name.description: The variant name used for this inference

staled_at.description: When the datapoint was marked as stale (if ever)

raw.description:
This is never omitted from the response even if it's None. A `null` value indicates no output from the model.
It's rare and unexpected from the model, but it's possible.

parsed.description: This is never omitted from the response even if it's None.

JsonMode.description:
This type is used to determine how to enforce JSON mode for a given variant.
Variants represent JSON mode in a slightly more abstract sense than ModelInferenceRequests, as
we support coercing tool calls into JSON mode.
This is represented as a tool config in the

ListDatasetsResponse.description: Response containing a list of datasets.

ListEvaluationRunsResponse.description: Response containing a list of evaluation runs.

ListFunctionsWithInferenceCountResponse.description: Response containing all functions with their inference counts

functions.description: List of functions with their inference counts, ordered by most recent inference

ListInferenceMetadataResponse.description: Response containing a list of inference metadata

ListInferencesRequest.description:
Request to list inferences with pagination and filters.
Used by the `POST /v1/inferences/list_inferences` endpoint.

function_name.description:
Optional function name to filter inferences by.
If provided, only inferences from this function will be returned.

variant_name.description:
Optional variant name to filter inferences by.
If provided, only inferences from this variant will be returned.

episode_id.description:
Optional episode ID to filter inferences by.
If provided, only inferences from this episode will be returned.

output_source.description:
Source of the inference output. Determines whether to return the original
inference output or demonstration feedback (manually-curated output) if available.
Defaults to `Inference` if not specified.

limit.description:
The maximum number of inferences to return.
Defaults to 20.

offset.description:
The number of inferences to skip before starting to return results.
Defaults to 0.

before.description:
Optional inference ID to paginate before (exclusive).
Returns inferences with IDs before this one (earlier in time).
Cannot be used together with `after` or `offset`.

after.description:
Optional inference ID to paginate after (exclusive).
Returns inferences with IDs after this one (later in time).
Cannot be used together with `before` or `offset`.

oneOf.description:
Optional filter to apply when querying inferences.
Supports filtering by metrics, tags, time, and logical combinations (AND/OR/NOT).

order_by.description:
Optional ordering criteria for the results.
Supports multiple sort criteria (e.g., sort by timestamp then by metric).

search_query_experimental.description:
Text query to filter. Case-insensitive substring search over the inferences' input and output.

THIS FEATURE IS EXPERIMENTAL, and we may change or remove it at any time.
We recommend against depending on this feature for critical use cases.

Important limitations:
- This requires an exact substring match; we do not tokenize this query string.
- This doesn't search for any content in the template itself.
- Quality is based on term frequency > 0, without any relevance scoring.
- There are no performance guarantees (it's best effort only). Today, with no other
  filters, it will perform a full table scan, which may be extremely slow depending
  on the data volume.

ListWorkflowEvaluationRunEpisodesByTaskN.description:
Response containing lists of workflow evaluation run episodes grouped by task name.

Each inner Vec contains all episodes that share the same task_name (or NULL task_name).
Episodes with NULL task_name are grouped individually.

ListWorkflowEvaluationRunsResponse.description: Response containing a list of workflow evaluation runs with episode counts.

oneOf.description:
The type of metric (boolean, float, demonstration).
None if the metric is not in the current config (e.g., was deleted).

MetricsWithFeedbackResponse.description: Response containing metrics with feedback statistics

ModelInference.description: Wire type for a single ModelInference (raw request and response sent to a model).

raw_request.description: Raw request sent to the model.

raw_response.description: Raw response received from the model.

model_name.description: Name of the model used for the inference.

model_provider_name.description: Name of the model provider used for the inference.

input_tokens.description:
Number of input tokens used for the inference.
This may be missing if the model inference provider does not report token usage.

output_tokens.description:
Number of output tokens used for the inference.
This may be missing if the model inference provider does not report token usage.

ttft_ms.description: Time to first token in milliseconds.

system.description: System prompt used for the inference.

input_messages.description: Input messages sent to the model.

output.description: Output content blocks from the model.

MultipleChoiceAnswer.description: A user's answer to a multiple choice question.

MultipleChoiceOption.description: An option in a multiple choice question.

label.description: The display text for this option that the user will see and select. Should be concise (1-5 words).

description.description: Explanation of what this option means or what will happen if chosen.

MultipleChoiceQuestion.description: A multiple choice question with options.

multi_select.description: Set to true to allow the user to select multiple options instead of just one.

ObjectStorageError.description:
A file that we failed to read from object storage.
This struct can NOT be stored in the database.

ObjectStorageFile.description:
A file stored in an object storage backend, with data.
This struct can NOT be stored in the database.
Note: `File` supports both `ObjectStorageFilePointer` and `ObjectStorageFile`.

ObjectStoragePointer.description:
A file stored in an object storage backend, without data.
This struct can be stored in the database. It's used by `StoredFile` (`StoredInput`).
Note: `File` supports both `ObjectStorageFilePointer` and `ObjectStorageFile`.

OpenAICustomTool.description:
`OpenAICustomTool` represents OpenAI's custom tool format, which allows
for text or grammar-based tool definitions beyond standard function calling.
Currently, this type is a wire + outbound + storage type so it forces a consistent format.
This only applies to the Chat Completions API. The Responses API has a slightly different request
shape so we implement a conversion in `responses.rs`.

OptimizationWorkflowSideInfo.description: Side info for optimization workflow tool (hidden from LLM).

max_wait_secs.description: Maximum time to wait for completion in seconds (default: 86400 = 24 hours).

allOf.description: The property to order by.

OrderBy.description: Order by clauses for querying inferences.

oneOf.description: Creation timestamp of the item.
Value of a metric.

name.description: The name of the metric to order by.

oneOf.description:
Relevance score of the search query in the input and output of the item.
Requires a search query (experimental). If it's not provided, we return an error.

NOTE: Relevance ordering is not yet implemented for Postgres and currently
falls back to id ordering. See TODO(#6441).

OrderByTerm.description:
The property to order by.
This is flattened in the public API inside the `OrderBy` struct.

RawResponseEntry.description:
A single entry in the raw response array, representing raw response data from one model inference.
This preserves the original provider-specific response string that TensorZero normalizes.

RawText.description:
Struct that represents raw text content that should be passed directly to the model
without any template processing or validation

RawUsageEntry.description:
A single entry in the raw usage array, representing usage data from one model inference.
This preserves the original provider-specific usage object for fields that TensorZero
normalizes away (e.g., OpenAI's `reasoning_tokens`, Anthropic's `cache_read_input_tokens`).

ResolveUuidResponse.description: Response type for the resolve_uuid endpoint.

ResolvedObject.description: A single resolved object type for a given UUID.

SearchEvaluationRunResult.description: A single search result for an evaluation run.

SearchEvaluationRunsResponse.description: Response containing search results for evaluation runs.

SearchWorkflowEvaluationRunsResponse.description: Response containing a list of workflow evaluation runs from search.

ServiceTier.description:
Service tier for inference requests.

Controls the priority and latency characteristics of the request.
Different providers map these values differently to their own service tiers.

Session.description: A session representing an autopilot conversation.

StatusUpdate.description: A status update within a session.

StorageKind.description:
Configuration for the object storage backend
Currently, we only support S3-compatible object storage and local filesystem storage
We test against Amazon S3, GCS, Cloudflare R2, and Minio

StoragePath.description:
Path to a file in an object storage backend.
This is part of the public API for `File`s. In particular, this is useful for roundtripping
unresolved inputs from stored inferences or datapoints, without requiring clients to fetch
file data first.

StoredChatInference.description: Wire variant of StoredChatInference for API responses with Python/TypeScript bindings

StoredContentBlock.description:
The version of `ContentBlock` that is stored in ClickHouse.
This is almost identical to `ContentBlock`, but without `File` data.

StoredFile.description:
A newtype wrapper around `ObjectStoragePointer` that handles legacy deserialization formats.
See the deserializer implementation below for details on the legacy formats it supports.

StoredInference.description:
Wire variant of StoredInference for API responses with Python/TypeScript bindings
This one should be used in all public interfaces

StoredInput.description:
The input type that we directly store in ClickHouse.
This is almost identical to `ResolvedInput`, but without `File` data.
Only the object-storage path is actually stored in clickhouse
(which can be used to re-fetch the file and produce a `ResolvedInput`).

`StoredInputMessage` has a custom deserializer that addresses legacy data formats in the database.

StoredInputMessage.description: `StoredInputMessage` has a custom deserializer that addresses legacy data formats in the database (see below).

StoredRequestMessage.description:
The message type that we directly store in ClickHouse.
This is almost identical to `RequestMessage`, but without `File` data.
Only the object-storage path is actually stored in clickhouse
The `RequestMessage/StoredRequestMessage` pair is the model-level equivalent
of `ResolvedInput/StoredInput`

Text.description:
InputMessages are validated against the input schema of the Function
and then templated and transformed into RequestMessages for a particular Variant.
They might contain tool calls or tool results along with text.
The abstraction we use to represent this is ContentBlock, which is a union of Text, ToolCall, and ToolResult.
ContentBlocks are collected into RequestMessages.
These RequestMessages are collected into a ModelInferenceRequest,
which should contain all information needed by a ModelProvider to perform the
inference that is called for.

Thought.description: Struct that represents a model's reasoning

signature.description:
An optional signature - used with Anthropic and OpenRouter for multi-turn
reasoning conversations. Other providers will ignore this field.

provider_type.description:
When set, this `Thought` block will only be used for providers
matching this type (e.g. `anthropic`). Other providers will emit
a warning and discard the block.

extra_data.description:
Provider-specific opaque data for multi-turn reasoning support.
For example, OpenRouter stores encrypted reasoning blocks with `{"format": "...", "encrypted": true}` structure.
Note: Not exposed to Python because `Value` doesn't implement `IntoPyObject`.

Tool.description:
`Tool` is the generic form for all tools that TensorZero itself manages.
This includes function tools (the original kind) and OpenAI's custom tools
(which support text and grammar formats). Future additions may include MCP and other standards.

We store this type (serialized) in the Array(String) in the `dynamic_tools` column
in the ChatInference, ChatInferenceDatapoint, and BatchModelInference tables.

For the wire format, we use `DynamicTool` which wraps this enum with a custom deserializer
that allows function tools to be specified without type tags for backward compatibility,
while other tool types require explicit tagging.

Notably, provider tools (like OpenAI websearch) are not part of this enum
as there's not really anything we can do besides experiment with them.
They are a separate type `ProviderTool`.

ToolCallWrapper.description:
`ToolCallWrapper` helps us disambiguate between `ToolCall` (no `raw_*`) and `InferenceResponseToolCall` (has `raw_*`).
Typically tool calls come from previous inferences and are therefore outputs of TensorZero (`InferenceResponseToolCall`)
but they may also be constructed client side or through the OpenAI endpoint `ToolCall` so we support both via this wrapper.

oneOf.description: Forces the LLM to call a specific tool. The String is the name of the tool.

specific.description: Forces the LLM to call a specific tool. The String is the name of the tool.

ToolChoice.description:
Most inference providers allow the user to force a tool to be used
and even specify which tool to be used.

This enum is used to denote this tool choice.

oneOf.title: ToolOutcomeRejected
oneOf.description:
The user rejected the tool call request
Note that this is currently never directly sent by the client - instead,
`ToolCallAuthorizationStatus::Rejected` is sent to the server.
The rejected tool will show in in the events list as `EventPayload::ToolResult`
with `ToolOutcome::Rejected`

error.description:
Structured error data from the tool.
For autopilot tools, this is typically a serialized `AutopilotToolError`
with a `kind` field discriminator (e.g., "ClientError", "Validation").

ToolResult.description: A ToolResult is the outcome of a ToolCall, which we may want to present back to the model

TopKEvaluationVisualization.description: Visualization data for a top-k evaluation.

variant_summaries.description: Map of variant names to their summary statistics.

confident_top_k_sizes.description:
Sizes k where we can confidently identify a top-k set.
For example, [2, 5] means there's statistical separation after the 2nd
and 5th ranked variants (sorted by lower confidence bound descending).

summary_text.description: Explanation of the results for the user.

UiConfig.description:
Response type for GET /internal/ui_config

Contains only UI-safe fields from the gateway config, excluding sensitive
information like provider credentials, API keys, and internal settings.

UnfilteredInferenceExtraBody.description:
The 'InferenceExtraBody' options provided directly in an inference request.
These have not yet been filtered by variant name

UnfilteredInferenceExtraHeaders.description:
The 'InferenceExtraHeaders' options provided directly in an inference request
These have not yet been filtered by variant name

Unknown.description:
Struct that represents an unknown provider-specific content block.
We pass this along as-is without any validation or transformation.

data.description: The underlying content block to be passed to the model provider.

model_name.description: A model name in your configuration (e.g. `my_gpt_5`) or a short-hand model name (e.g. `openai::gpt-5`)

provider_name.description: A provider name for the model you specified (e.g. `my_openai`)

allOf.description: Datapoint tool parameters.
Metadata fields to update.

id.description: The ID of the datapoint to update. Required.

oneOf.description: Datapoint input. If omitted, it will be left unchanged.

output.description:
Chat datapoint output. If omitted, it will be left unchanged. If specified as `null`, it will be set to
`null`. Otherwise, it will overwrite the existing output (and can be an empty array).

oneOf.description:
DEPRECATED (#4725 / 2026.2+): Datapoint tool parameters.
Moving forward, don't nest these fields.

tags.description:
Datapoint tags. If omitted, it will be left unchanged. If empty, it will be cleared. Otherwise,
it will be overwrite the existing tags.

oneOf.description:
DEPRECATED (#4725 / 2026.2+): Metadata fields to update.
Moving forward, don't nest these fields.

UpdateChatDatapointRequest.description: An update request for a chat datapoint.

allOf.description: Metadata fields to update.

id.description: The ID of the datapoint to update. Required.

UpdateDatapointMetadataRequest.description: A request to update the metadata of a single datapoint.

allOf.description: Request to update a chat datapoint.

oneOf.title: UpdateDatapointRequestChat
oneOf.description: Request to update a chat datapoint.

allOf.description: Request to update a JSON datapoint.

oneOf.title: UpdateDatapointRequestJson
oneOf.description: Request to update a JSON datapoint.

UpdateDatapointRequest.description: A tagged request to update a single datapoint in a dataset.

UpdateDatapointsResponse.description: A response to a request to update one or more datapoints in a dataset.

ids.description:
The IDs of the datapoints that were updated.
These are newly generated IDs for UpdateDatapoint requests, and they are the same IDs for UpdateDatapointMetadata requests.

UpdateDynamicToolParamsRequest.description: A request to update the dynamic tool parameters of a datapoint.

allowed_tools.description:
A subset of static tools configured for the function that the inference is explicitly allowed to use.
If omitted, it will be left unchanged. If specified as `null`, it will be cleared (we allow function-configured tools plus additional tools
provided at inference time). If specified as a value, it will be set to the provided value.

additional_tools.description:
Tools that the user provided at inference time (not in function config), in addition to the function-configured tools, that are also allowed.
Modifying `additional_tools` DOES NOT automatically modify `allowed_tools`; `allowed_tools` must be explicitly updated to include
new tools or exclude removed tools.
If omitted, it will be left unchanged. If specified as a value, it will be set to the provided value.

oneOf.description:
User-specified tool choice strategy.
If omitted, it will be left unchanged. If specified as `null`, we will clear the dynamic tool choice and use function-configured tool choice.

parallel_tool_calls.description:
Whether to use parallel tool calls in the inference.
If omitted, it will be left unchanged. If specified as `null`, it will be set to `null`. If specified as a value, it will be set to the provided value.

provider_tools.description:
Provider-specific tool configurations
If omitted, it will be left unchanged. If specified as a value, it will be set to the provided value.

allOf.description: Metadata fields to update.

id.description: The ID of the datapoint to update. Required.

oneOf.description: Datapoint input. If omitted, it will be left unchanged.
JSON datapoint output. If omitted, it will be left unchanged. If `null`, it will be set to `null`. If specified as a value, it will be set to the provided value.

output_schema.description:
The output schema of the JSON datapoint. If omitted, it will be left unchanged. If specified as `null`, it will be set to `null`. If specified as a value, it will be set to the provided value.
If not provided, the function's output schema will be used.

tags.description:
Datapoint tags. If omitted, it will be left unchanged. If empty, it will be cleared. Otherwise,
it will be overwrite the existing tags.

oneOf.description:
DEPRECATED (#4725 / 2026.2+): Metadata fields to update.
Moving forward, don't nest these fields.

UpdateJsonDatapointRequest.description: An update request for a JSON datapoint.

UrlFile.description: A file that can be located at a URL

UserQuestionAnswer.description: A user's response to a question.

VariantPerformanceRow.description:
Row returned from the variant performance query.
Contains statistics for each (variant, time_period) combination.

period_start.description:
Start datetime of the period in RFC 3339 format.
For cumulative time window, this is '1970-01-01T00:00:00Z'.

count.description: Number of data points in this (variant, period) combination

ci_error.description:
95% confidence interval error margin (1.96 * stdev / sqrt(count))
Null if count < 2 (when stdev is null)

performances.description: Performance statistics for each (variant, time_period) combination

VariantSummary.description: Summary statistics for a variant's performance.

failed.description: Whether this variant failed during evaluation.

VariantThroughput.description: Row returned from the get_function_throughput_by_variant query.

period_start.description: Start datetime of the period in RFC 3339 format with milliseconds

count.description: Number of inferences for this (period, variant) combination

allOf.description: Top-k evaluation results showing variant performance comparisons.

oneOf.title: VisualizationTypeTopKEvaluation
oneOf.description: Top-k evaluation results showing variant performance comparisons.

oneOf.title: VisualizationTypeUnknown
oneOf.description:
Unknown visualization type for forward compatibility.
Old clients can gracefully handle new visualization types they don't recognize.

VisualizationType.description: Types of visualizations that can be displayed.

WorkflowEvaluationProject.description: Information about a single workflow evaluation project.

WorkflowEvaluationRun.description: Information about a single workflow evaluation run.

WorkflowEvaluationRunEpisodeWithFeedback.description: Information about a single workflow evaluation run episode with feedback.

feedback_metric_names.description: The feedback metric names, sorted alphabetically.

feedback_values.description: The feedback values, corresponding to the metric names.

WorkflowEvaluationRunStatistics.description: Statistics for a single metric within a workflow evaluation run.

WorkflowEvaluationRunWithEpisodeCount.description: Information about a single workflow evaluation run with episode count.

WriteConfigResponse.description: Response from writing a config snapshot.

hash.description: The hash identifying this config version.
